furnitureContract.dimensions: Every furniture object declares width, depth and height in tile units. Dimensions describe the visual envelope; occupancy remains the smaller walk-collision footprint.
furnitureContract.anchors: Place an object's origin at its back-left floor corner. Supply a front layer whenever the visual envelope can cover the actor.

floor.repeat: seamless square material; the renderer maps it onto a diamond floor tile
floor.prompt: Use case: stylized-concept. Asset type: seamless material source for an orthographic isometric pixel room. Primary request: warm honey oak floorboards, straight parallel boards, no perspective. Style/medium: clean 2.5D pixel art at a consistent low pixel density. Composition/framing: a perfectly flat square top-down texture with edge-to-edge continuity. Lighting/mood: neutral even lighting only. Constraints: seamless on all four edges, no furniture, no wall, no cast shadow, no text, no border, no watermark. Avoid: diagonal camera view, room render, gradient lighting, individual loose objects.

wall.repeat: seamless vertical material; the renderer maps it onto an upright wall face
wall.prompt: Use case: stylized-concept. Asset type: seamless wall material source for an orthographic isometric pixel room. Primary request: warm off-white plaster with sparse hand-painted pixel texture, front-on and perfectly flat. Style/medium: clean 2.5D pixel art. Composition/framing: a rectangular, edge-to-edge repeat texture with no perspective. Lighting/mood: neutral even lighting only. Constraints: seamless left/right and top/bottom, no window, no baseboard, no furniture, no shadow, no text, no watermark. Avoid: a rendered room, angled wall, vanishing point, gradients.

generationSequence: Generate only a flat material or one furniture face against a removable chroma-key background; never generate a complete room.
Inspect the material at native resolution and test it in a 2x2 repeat before accepting it.
Generate furniture faces separately using the declared width/depth/height and named layer role.
Compile and inspect behind/front actor audits before promoting an asset from draft.
